ocr.text: In examining the treatment modalities utilized within the analyzed patient cohort, the data presented in the table elucidates
significant trends in non-operative management approaches. Specifically, the non-operative treatment modality accounted for
96 patients, representing 22.7% of the entire cohort of 25,629 individuals. This subset illustrates a notable demographic
composition, with a gender distribution of 76 females and 20 males, highlighting a potential area for further investigation into
gender-specific responses to treatment.
Treatment modality  No. of patients  % of entire cohort (%)  Genderm = male  Mean age (range) years  Fracture types (% within the treatment group)
Non-operative  96  22.7  76 F20 M  70.5(43–102)  n = 25 isolated tuberosity fractures (26%)n = 24 subcapital 2-part fractures (25%)n = 32 3-part fractures (33.3%)n = 15 complex fractures (15.6%)
Locked nailing  45  10.6  32 F13 M  74(31–92)  n = 23 subcapital 2-part fractures (51.1%)n = 20 3-part fractures (44.4%)n = 2 complex fractures (4.4%)
Locked plating  211  49.9  148 F63 M  64.4(28–92)  n = 4 isolated tuberosity fractures (1.9%)n = 11 subcapital 2-part fractures (5.2%)n = 119 3-part fractures (56.4%)n = 77 complex fractures (36.5%)
Hemi-fracture prosthesis  29  6.9  19 F10 M  64.7(43–84)  n = 3 3-part fractures (10.3%)n = 26 complex fractures (89.7%)
Reverse fracture prosthesis  42  9.9  37 F5 M  78.9(62–95)  n = 4 3-part fractures (9.5%)n = 38 complex fractures (90.5%)

Hospital LOS (Days)  27.2 ± 26.6 (21)
Additionally, the mean age of patients receiving non-operative treatment was recorded at 70.5 years, with a range spanning
from 43 to 102 years. The fracture types within this group were categorized, emphasizing the diversity of injuries managed non-
operatively. The baseline and 12-week metrics of the cohort are essential for understanding the efficacy of treatments and
tracking progress over time, particularly regarding the changes in *TCC levels, which are critical for assessing patient
outcomes. This data underscores the importance of tailored treatment strategies in managing complex fracture cases in older
populations.
